root.narration: You are dribbling the ball up the court. A teammate is wide open near the basket, but you are staring down at the ball while you dribble. What do you do?

choices.text: Keep your head up and look around while dribbling so you can see your open teammate
Stop dribbling and then look around for someone to pass to
Keep staring at the ball and dribble all the way to the basket yourself

outcome.headline: Great court vision!
outcome.explanation: By keeping your eyes up while dribbling, you spotted your open teammate and made a perfect pass for an easy basket. Good ball handlers can dribble without looking down.
outcome.whatToRemember: Practice dribbling without looking at the ball. Your eyes should always be scanning the court for open teammates.

keyTerms.definition: The ability to see the whole court and know where all the players are while you handle the ball

outcome.headline: You found the pass, but it took a moment
outcome.explanation: Stopping your dribble to look around works, but now you cannot dribble again. A defender might have time to close in on your teammate before you pass.
outcome.whatToRemember: Try to keep your dribble alive while you look around. Once you pick up the ball, your options get smaller.

outcome.headline: You missed the open player!
outcome.explanation: Because you were staring at the ball, you never saw your teammate waving for a pass. You dribbled into traffic and a defender stole the ball.
outcome.whatToRemember: Basketball is a team sport. Keep your head up so you can see the whole court and find open teammates.
